mainEntity.name: Frequently Asked Questions: Workplace Injury Settlements and Rights
mainEntity.text: Frequently Asked Questions: Workplace Injury Settlements and Rights

acceptedAnswer.text:
### Understanding Your Rights After a Struck-By or Chemical Injury
At Just Work Comp Law, we hear the same worries from injured workers every day. Here are the most critical answers you need to navigate the workers' compensation system in Utah, Idaho, and beyond.

**Q: Can I really get workers' comp if the accident was my fault?**
**A:** Yes. Workers' compensation is a no-fault system. Whether you dropped the tool on yourself or stepped into a hazardous area, you are covered as long as you were at work and not intoxicated.

**Q: How much will I actually receive for my lost wages?**
**A:** In most jurisdictions we serve, including Salt Lake City and Boise, you receive roughly 66% (two-thirds) of your average weekly wage. This is non-taxable income. There are caps based on the state's "average" wage, which we can help you calculate.

**Q: What if the insurance company denies my surgery?**
**A:** This is a common tactic. They use "Paper Doctors" to say the surgery isn't necessary. We can file an "Application for Hearing" to have a judge order the insurance company to pay for the medical care your real doctor says you need.

**Q: Can I choose my own doctor?**
**A:** This depends on the state. In Utah, you generally have the right to choose your doctor after the first visit. In other states, you might be restricted to a "panel" of doctors. Choosing the right doctor is the most important decision you will make in your case.

**Q: Does it cost anything to hire an attorney?**
**A:** No. We work on a **contingency fee basis**. This means we only get paid if we win your case or secure a settlement. Our fee is a percentage of the back-pay or settlement we recover for you, and it is usually regulated by state law.

### Why Choose Just Work Comp Law?
We aren't a "settlement mill." Our attorneys, including Blair Jones and Jon Bonnesen, pride themselves on litigation excellence. If the insurance company won't play fair, we are prepared to take your case to a full hearing. We focus on the relationship, ensuring you are never left in the dark about your case status.

If you are hurting after a workplace accident, don't wait for the insurance company to do the right thing—they won't. Call Just Work Comp Law at 801-746-0290 for immediate action.
